general_feedback: The single documentation addition for `@tparam C` ('the type of the sequence itself') is accurate, concise, consistent with the surrounding `@tparam A` and `@tparam CC` entries, and follows Scaladoc conventions (lowercase start, no trailing punctuation). It correctly describes the role of the `C` type parameter as the self-type of the sequence used as a return type in operations like `distinctBy`, `diff`, and `intersect`.
